title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: New York, NY (with Tel Aviv HQ collaboration)
description: Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role builds and scales the operational backbone of Finout's go-to-market engine. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, with experience scaling revenue operations beyond $30M+ ARR. Deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise needed, plus cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Finout is a cloud cost-management company with 100+ people across Tel Aviv and New York, trusted by brands like The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, Fintech/SaaS expertise aligns perfectly with Finout's cloud cost-management focus, Salesforce admin background directly addresses core need, and experience scaling revenue operations matches the hands-on leadership scope. Location is viable given Tel Aviv HQ collaboration and remote flexibility.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed Finout is building out its RevOps function under a CRO—this is exactly the scaling moment where your 15 years in B2B SaaS and Salesforce expertise would have immediate impact. Given Finout's growth trajectory (100+ team, $30M+ ARR companies in your client base) and your track record managing GTM operations across Finance, Sales, and Marketing, you'd be walking into a role where day-one credibility matters. The Tel Aviv HQ connection is a bonus—have you considered a New York-based role with that home-market overlap?

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: This role accelerates revenue growth through data-driven investment strategy, scalable territory design, and consultative partnership with business leaders. Responsibilities include maintaining territory mapping in CRM systems (Fullcast, Salesforce), leading market expansion and rep capacity modeling, and partnering with Sales leadership and Finance. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations or Sales Strategy, deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, strong analytical skills with Excel, SQL, and BI tools (Tableau, Retool), and Salesforce proficiency. Gong builds revenue intelligence technology.
score_reason: Omri is a senior RevOps executive with 15+ years B2B SaaS experience, exceeding the 3-5 year requirement with strong Salesforce proficiency, analytical expertise (Excel, SQL, BI tools), and deep GTM knowledge—all core to this territory strategy role. Gong's revenue intelligence focus aligns perfectly with his data-driven pipeline forecasting and CAC/LTV optimization background.
ai_opener: Hi there – I've been impressed by Gong's approach to revenue intelligence and how it's reshaping B2B sales operations. With 15+ years building RevOps functions at scale in SaaS, I've led territory strategy, pipeline forecasting, and rep capacity modeling across multiple growth stages—including Salesforce architecture and BI tool implementation. I'd love to explore how I could help Gong accelerate revenue growth through data-driven territory design and scalable operations.

title: GTM Engineer
description: As Nooks' first GTM Engineer, you'll serve as the technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization, owning setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform. Nooks is an AI Sales Assistant Platform that automates busywork so reps can focus on selling, loved by teams at HubSpot, Rippling, and hundreds more. The company has raised over $70M from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins and has grown ARR 4x then 3x. Ideal for technical builders excited about sales automation.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with GTM Engineer role, AI-native platform focus, and RevOps technical expertise. Strong fit for first GTM Engineer at well-funded B2B SaaS with proven sales automation traction, though role emphasizes technical building over broader revenue operations strategy.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years building revenue operations and GTM infrastructure at scale. I'm drawn to Nooks because you're solving a real problem in the sales stack with proven adoption (HubSpot, Rippling). Having managed complex sales automation implementations and SDR team enablement, I'd love to explore how my experience optimizing sales workflows and platform implementations could help you build out the GTM engineering function as you scale.

title: GTM Engineer
description: FacilityOS is a B2B SaaS company helping mid-to-large enterprises stay safe, compliant, and operationally sharp through its facility management platform. As GTM Engineer, you'll build, configure, and continuously improve a collection of intelligent AI agents that help Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success work smarter. You'll work at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, designing how their AI systems connect, run, learn, and improve over time. Strong fit for those who enjoy building AI-driven GTM systems.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, B2B SaaS, AI-native systems, and remote setup. Excellent fit for someone with RevOps/GTM background building intelligent automation across Sales/Marketing/CS workflows. Only minor concern is the role's technical depth may skew more engineering-focused than typical RevOps executive path.
ai_opener: FacilityOS's vision of AI agents coordinating Sales, Marketing, and CS workflows is exactly where modern GTM is headed. With 15+ years building data-driven revenue systems and pipeline intelligence in B2B SaaS, I'm drawn to roles where I can architect how these systems actually connect to human workflows—not just data plumbing. Your GTM Engineer role sits at that intersection, and I'd love to explore how my experience scaling GTM operations through intelligent automation could help your team ship faster.

title: GTM Engineer

score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer role, RevOps focus, and PLG-to-revenue optimization. Supabase's developer-first platform and async structure match Omri's B2B SaaS expertise, though the role is slightly more execution-focused than typical head-of-function level.

Your async, PLG-to-revenue model reminds me of the most efficient growth teams I've worked with, and I'd be curious to explore how we could accelerate your pipeline velocity and conversion efficiency.
